mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth combined with low unemployment signals robust labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or pairwise correlation between PAYEMS/UNRATE drops below 0.5 in 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in residential construction permits signals weakening housing demand, which predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as economic activity slows.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or HOUST reverses decline within 15 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing activity, which predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as corporate profits rise.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or INDPRO fails to sustain 1.5% increase for 10 consecutive days.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in inflation expectations (CPI) above 2.5% predicts central bank tightening, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as bond yields rise.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or CPIAUCSL fails to sustain 2.5% level for 15 consecutive days.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in consumer spending (RSAFS) and GDP (GDPC1) predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as aggregate demand strengthens.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or RSAFS/GDPC1 diverge by > 10% in 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in unemployment (UNRATE) above 5.5% predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as recession risks rise.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or UNRATE reverses increase within 10 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 110 predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as manufacturing activity contracts.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or INDPRO fails to sustain 110 level for 15 consecutive days.
